title: Cerumen impaction

red_flags.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped sudden or rapid hearing change and neurological warning fields in adults.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment

examination.text: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.
examination.rationale: Supports clinician-observed impaction and management-modifying history or examination; no treatment item is mapped.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.

exact_section.heading: Diagnosis and modifying factors
exact_section.locator: Key Action Statements 2A–2B; official full-text lines 302–305 and 417–528

investigations.text: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped cerumen identity, hearing, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, laterality, otoscopy, ear-canal, tympanic-membrane, and impaction fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

exact_section.heading: Scope, definition, and symptoms of cerumen impaction
exact_section.locator: Full guideline supporting text and Key Action Statement 2A; official full-text lines 183–217 and 417–475

investigations.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped hearing-screen, audiology, hearing-aid discussion, questions, and clinician-arranged audiology documentation in adults.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

referral.text: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician referral after unsuccessful management; broad or fixed follow-up fields are not mapped.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.

exact_section.heading: Outcomes assessment and referral
exact_section.locator: Key Action Statements 9–10; official full-text lines 315–316 and 1137 onward

referral.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped tinnitus, vertigo, pain, discharge, and abnormal-ear context; overlapping fields use the cerumen source.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source_limitations: Noise exposure, recent trauma, tuning-fork testing, prior wax removal or drops, mastoid, throat, tonsil, lymph-node, neck, nasal, sinus, previous-record, generic management, general safety-netting, fixed two-week review, and combined history-draft fields remain unsupported.
The workflow must not assume wax explains hearing loss, tinnitus, pain, discharge, or vertigo before clinician examination.
Removal method, drops, irrigation, hearing-aid selection, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
